bird.name: Grey butcherbird
bird.summary: The grey butcherbird (Cracticus torquatus) is a widely distributed species endemic to Australia. It occurs in a range of different habitats…

relations.subject: Grey butcherbird
relations.evidence: The nest is lined with grasses and other soft fibres. Nests are normally located within off the ground. The grey butcherbird is found in a range of habitats, from eucalypt forests and woodlands, mallee and acacia shrublands, rainforests and riparian vegetation to urban areas and residential townships. The denser forests tend to be the favour for inland areas
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。
